job: Configure cluster using GitOps declarative approach
user_story: When managing OpenShift cluster configurations, I want to use GitOps to deploy and sync cluster-wide resources from Git repositories, so I can maintain consistency and version control for cluster settings.
section: Configuring an OpenShift cluster by deploying an application with cluster configurations
evidence: With GitOps, you can configure Argo CD to recursively sync the content of a Git directory with an application that contains custom configurations for your cluster.
notes: Core job for declarative cluster configuration management

parent_job: Configure cluster using GitOps declarative approach
job: Set up cluster-scoped Argo CD instance
user_story: When setting up GitOps for cluster management, I want to configure Argo CD with cluster-scoped permissions, so it can manage cluster-wide resources like operators and RBAC.
section: Using an Argo CD instance to manage cluster-scoped resources
evidence: Update the existing Subscription object for the GitOps Operator and add the namespace of the Argo CD instance to the ARGOCD_CLUSTER_CONFIG_NAMESPACES environment variable
notes: Requires cluster-admin privileges; includes security warning about privilege escalation

parent_job: Configure cluster using GitOps declarative approach
job: Create and sync cluster configuration applications
user_story: When deploying cluster configurations, I want to create Argo CD applications that sync Git repository contents to the cluster, so configuration changes are automatically applied.
section: Creating an application by using the Argo CD dashboard / oc tool / GitOps CLI
evidence: Create cluster-configs application with repository URL, path cluster, namespace spring-petclinic, directory recurse checked
notes: Supports multiple creation methods: dashboard, oc CLI, argocd CLI (default and core mode)

parent_job: Configure cluster using GitOps declarative approach
user_story: When cluster configurations change in Git, I want to synchronize them to the cluster either manually or automatically, so the cluster state matches the desired state in Git.
section: Synchronizing your application with your Git repository
evidence: Click SYNC on the cluster-configs tile, review the changes, and then click SYNCHRONIZE. You can modify the synchronization policy for Argo CD to automatically apply changes
notes: Supports manual sync policy (default) and automatic sync policy

parent_job: Configure cluster using GitOps declarative approach
job: Run Argo CD on infrastructure nodes
user_story: When managing Argo CD deployment, I want to run Argo CD instances on infrastructure nodes with appropriate tolerations, so workloads are properly isolated and scheduled.
section: Running the Argo CD instance at the cluster-level
evidence: Add the runOnInfra toggle in the GitOpsService custom resource with optional tolerations for tainted infrastructure nodes
notes: Includes node labeling and optional taints for workload isolation

job: Manage RBAC permissions for cluster-scoped Argo CD instances
user_story: When operating Argo CD at cluster scope, I want to control and customize permissions for cluster resources, so I can grant appropriate access while maintaining security boundaries.
section: Default permissions / Adding permissions / Customizing permissions
evidence: Argo CD instance has admin privileges in its namespace and cluster-wide read privileges. You can extend permissions by creating cluster roles and bindings.
notes: Covers default permissions, in-built permissions, and extension mechanisms

parent_job: Manage RBAC permissions for cluster-scoped Argo CD instances
user_story: When deploying Argo CD, I want to understand its default permission model, so I can assess security implications and plan necessary customizations.
section: Default permissions of an Argo CD instance
evidence: Argo CD instance has admin privileges in the namespace where deployed and cluster-wide get/list/watch permissions on all resources
notes: Includes reference permissions for config.openshift.io, operators.coreos.com, user.openshift.io, storage.k8s.io, console.openshift.io

parent_job: Manage RBAC permissions for cluster-scoped Argo CD instances
job: Add additional permissions for cluster configuration
user_story: When Argo CD needs access to additional cluster resources, I want to create custom cluster roles and bindings, so Argo CD can manage those resources declaratively.
evidence: Create ClusterRole with additional rules (e.g., secrets permissions), then create ClusterRoleBinding for openshift-gitops-argocd-application-controller service account
notes: Requires cluster-admin access; permissions added via cluster role bindings to service accounts

parent_job: Manage RBAC permissions for cluster-scoped Argo CD instances
job: Create user-defined cluster roles for cluster-scoped instances
user_story: When default operator-managed permissions don't fit my requirements, I want to disable default cluster roles and create custom ones, so I can precisely control Argo CD's cluster access.
section: Customizing permissions by creating user-defined cluster roles
evidence: Set spec.defaultClusterScopedRoleDisabled to true in Argo CD CR, then create custom ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding resources
notes: Allows full customization; operator will not reconcile back custom roles

parent_job: Manage RBAC permissions for cluster-scoped Argo CD instances
user_story: When I want to extend Argo CD permissions without creating roles from scratch, I want to use aggregated cluster roles, so I can compose permissions from multiple role definitions.
section: Customizing permissions by creating aggregated cluster roles
evidence: Set spec.aggregatedClusterRoles to true in Argo CD CR; operator creates aggregated role with view and admin child roles
notes: Technology available in GitOps 1.14+; only works for Application Controller; includes aggregation rule with label selectors

parent_job: Manage RBAC permissions for cluster-scoped Argo CD instances
user_story: When I want to restrict Argo CD's resource discovery, I want to enable respectRBAC, so the controller only watches resources it has permissions to access.
evidence: Set spec.controller.respectRBAC to normal or strict in Argo CD resource; controller stops watching resources it cannot list or access
notes: Prevents errors during sync when controller lacks permissions; strict mode is more accurate but increases API calls

job: Install OLM Operators declaratively using GitOps
user_story: When managing operator lifecycle, I want to use GitOps to install and configure OLM operators from Git, so operator deployments are version-controlled and repeatable.
evidence: GitOps manages specific cluster-scoped resources and takes care of installing cluster Operators or namespace-scoped OLM Operators
notes: Covers both cluster-scoped and namespace-scoped operators

parent_job: Install OLM Operators declaratively using GitOps
user_story: When deploying cluster-scoped operators, I want to place Subscription resources in Git, so Argo CD automatically installs operators in the openshift-operators namespace.
evidence: Create Subscription resource for operators (e.g., Grafana) in Git repo; OLM uses default global-operators OperatorGroup
notes: No need to manage OperatorGroup for cluster-scoped operators

parent_job: Install OLM Operators declaratively using GitOps
user_story: When deploying namespace-scoped operators, I want to place Subscription and OperatorGroup resources in Git, so operators are installed in specific namespaces with proper scope.
evidence: Create Namespace, OperatorGroup, and Subscription resources (e.g., Ansible Automation Platform) in Git repository
notes: Important: only one OperatorGroup per namespace to avoid TooManyOperatorGroups error

job: Scale Argo CD Application Controller with cluster sharding
user_story: When Argo CD manages many clusters with high memory usage, I want to distribute clusters across multiple controller replicas using sharding, so resource usage is balanced and performance is optimized.
section: Sharding clusters across Argo CD Application Controller replicas
evidence: You can shard clusters across multiple Argo CD Application Controller replicas if the controller is managing too many clusters and uses too much memory
notes: Technology Preview feature; includes round-robin algorithm and dynamic scaling options

parent_job: Scale Argo CD Application Controller with cluster sharding
user_story: When enabling sharding, I want to use the round-robin algorithm instead of legacy hash-based, so clusters are distributed evenly across shards.
evidence: Set ARGOCD_CONTROLLER_SHARDING_ALGORITHM environment variable to round-robin; ensures balanced workload distribution and prevents shard overload
notes: Technology Preview; provides better distribution than legacy algorithm; requires sharding.enabled=true and replicas configuration

parent_job: Scale Argo CD Application Controller with cluster sharding
user_story: When cluster count fluctuates, I want shards to scale dynamically based on configured thresholds, so shard distribution remains optimal without manual intervention.
evidence: Set spec.controller.sharding.dynamicScalingEnabled to true with minShards, maxShards, and clustersPerShard parameters
notes: Technology Preview; automatic adjustment prevents manual shard count modifications; ensures well-balanced shards as clusters are added/removed

parent_job: Scale Argo CD Application Controller with cluster sharding
user_story: When sharding is enabled, I want to verify which clusters are assigned to which shards, so I can confirm even distribution and troubleshoot issues.
section: Enabling the round-robin sharding algorithm / Verification steps
evidence: Set log level to debug and search for 'processed by shard' messages to observe cluster-to-shard assignments
notes: Verification includes checking StatefulSet pod count, log messages for 'Using filter function: round-robin', and shard assignment distribution
